before.description: Recent notes show slower gait after lunch and near hallway turns.
Two recent evening notes include the same medication refusal phrase.

citations.quote: I do not want it tonight

before.description: Noise from the corridor was linked to a request for a quieter room.

after.description: Frequent evening medication refusals linked to noisier environments.

citations.quote: Aiko refused evening medication again around 19:40 when the corridor was noisy after the fire drill.
Resident again refused medication and repeated, 'I do not want it tonight.'
Aiko refused evening medication again around 19:40 when the corridor was noisy after the fire drill.
Resident again refused medication and repeated, 'I do not want it tonight.'

changes.rationale: Recent records indicate recurrent medication refusal episodes specifically tied to noisy evening environments, emphasizing the need to document this as a change in pattern.

before.situation: Medication round with unfamiliar staff or corridor noise present
before.approach: Give a short break before re-offering medication with a familiar staff member when possible.
Lower room noise, sit at eye level, and restart the explanation slowly.

after.situation: Evening medication round during noisy disturbances like fire drills
after.approach: Move resident to a quiet lounge, play favorite music, and offer medication with apple jelly.

citations.quote: She calmed down after staff moved her to the quiet lounge and played her favorite enka music, then took the medication with apple jelly.
She calmed down after staff moved her to the quiet lounge and played her favorite enka music, then took the medication with apple jelly.

changes.rationale: New observations suggest a specific approach of moving to a quieter environment with familiar calming elements effectively helps in medication administration during noisy periods.
